children.heading: .—Establishment and Availability of Fund
children.label: a .—Establishment and Availability of Fund
children.text: There is hereby established in the Treasury of the United States a special fund to be known as the “Judiciary Information Technology Fund” (hereafter in this section referred to as the “Fund”). Moneys in the Fund shall be available to the Director without fiscal year limitation for the procurement (by lease, purchase, exchange, transfer, or otherwise) of information technology resources for program activities included in the courts of appeals, district courts, and other judicial services account of the judicial branch of the United States. The Fund shall also be available for expenses, including personal services, support personnel in the courts and in the Administrative Office of the United States Courts, and other costs, for the effective management, coordination, operation, and use of information technology resources purchased by the Fund. In addition, all agencies of the judiciary may make deposits into the Fund to meet their information technology needs in accordance with subsections (b) and (c)(2).

children.heading: Plan for Meeting Information Technology Needs.—
children.label: b Plan for Meeting Information Technology Needs.—

children.heading: .—Development of plan
children.label: 1 .—Development of plan
children.text: The Director shall develop and annually revise, with the approval of the Judicial Conference of the United States, a long range plan for meeting the information technology resources needs of the activities funded under subsection (a) and shall include an annual estimate of any fees that may be collected under section 404 of the Judiciary Appropriations Act, 1991 (; ). Such plan and revisions shall be submitted to Congress.Public Law 101–515104 Stat. 2133

children.heading: .—Expenditures consistent with plan
children.label: 2 .—Expenditures consistent with plan
children.text: The Director may use amounts in the Fund to procure information technology resources for the activities funded under subsection (a) only in accordance with the plan developed under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: all proceeds resulting from activities conducted under subsection (a), including net proceeds of disposal of excess or surplus property, all fees collected after the date of the enactment of the Judicial Amendments Act of 1994 by the judiciary under section 404 of the Judiciary Appropriations Act, 1991 (; ) and receipts from carriers and others for loss of or damage to property;Public Law 101–515104 Stat. 2133

children.label: B
children.text: amounts available for activities described in subsection (a) from funds appropriated to the judiciary; and

children.label: C
children.text: any advances and reimbursements required by paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Advances and reimbursements
children.label: 2 .—Advances and reimbursements
children.text: Whenever the Director procures information technology resources for any entity in the judicial branch other than the courts or the Administrative Office, that entity shall advance or reimburse the Fund, whichever the Director considers appropriate, for the costs of the information technology resources, from appropriations available to that entity.

children.heading: .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.label: d .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Fund for any fiscal year such sums as are required to supplement amounts deposited under subsection (c) in order to conduct activities under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—For each fiscal year
children.label: 1 .—For each fiscal year
children.text: In fiscal year 1990, and in each succeeding fiscal year, the Director may enter into contracts for the procurement of information technology resources in amounts which, in the aggregate, do not exceed amounts estimated to be collected under subsection (c) for that fiscal year in advance of the availability of amounts in the Fund for such contracts.

children.label: A
children.text: funds are available and adequate for payment of the costs of such contract for the first fiscal year and for payment of any costs of cancellation or termination of the contract;

children.label: B
children.text: such contract is in accordance with the Director’s authority in section 604(g) of 28 U.S.C.; and,1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.label: i
children.text: the need for the information technology resources being provided will continue over the period of the contract; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of the multi-year contract will yield substantial cost savings when compared with other methods of providing the necessary resources.

children.heading: .—Cancellation costs of multiyear contract
children.label: 3 .—Cancellation costs of multiyear contract
children.text: Any cancellation costs incurred with respect to a contract entered into under paragraph (2) shall be paid from currently available amounts in the Fund.

children.heading: .—Authority of Administrator of General Services
children.label: f .—Authority of Administrator of General Services
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of the Administrator of General Services under sections 501–505 of title 40.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Director shall submit to the Congress an annual report on the operation of the Fund, including on the inventory, use, and acquisition of information technology resources from the Fund and the consistency of such acquisition with the plan prepared under subsection (b). The report shall set forth the amounts deposited into the Fund under subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: the specific actions taken and the progress made to improve the plan developed under subsection (b) and the long range automation plan and strategic business plan developed under subsection (k); and2See References in Text note below.2

children.label: B
children.text: a comparison of planned Fund expenditures and accomplishments with actual Fund expenditures and accomplishments, and the reasons for any delays in scheduled systems development, or budget overruns.

children.heading: .—Reprogramming
children.label: h .—Reprogramming
children.text: The Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts, under the supervision of the Judicial Conference of the United States, may transfer amounts up to $1,000,000 from the Fund into the account to which the funds were originally appropriated. Any amounts transferred from the Fund in excess of $1,000,000 in any fiscal year may only be transferred by following reprogramming procedures in compliance with section 606 of the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1989 (; ).Public Law 100–459102 Stat. 2227

children.heading: .—Appropriations Into the Fund
children.label: i .—Appropriations Into the Fund
children.text: If the budget request of the judiciary is appropriated in full, the amount deposited into the Fund during any fiscal year under the authority of subsection (c)(1)(B) will be the same as the amount of funds requested by the judiciary for activities described in subsection (a). If an amount to be deposited is not specified in statute by Congress and if the full request is not appropriated, the amount to be deposited under subsection (c)(1)(B) will be set by the spending priorities established by the Judicial Conference.

children.heading: .—Long Range Management and Business Plans
children.label: j .—Long Range Management and Business Plans

children.text: develop an overall strategic business plan which would identify the judiciary’s missions, goals, and objectives;
develop a long range automation plan based on the strategic business plan and user needs assessments;
establish effective Administrative Office oversight of court automation efforts to ensure the effective operation of existing systems and control over developments of future systems;
expedite efforts to complete the development and implementation of life cycle management standards;
utilize the standards in developing the next generation of case management and financial systems; and
assess the current utilization and future user requirements of the data communications network.
